text: Welcome everyone to this short talk about vector databases.
Today we will cover what embeddings are and why they matter.
An embedding is a list of numbers that represents meaning.
Similar pieces of text end up close together in that space.
A vector database stores millions of these embeddings.
The core operation is similarity search over the vectors.
Exact search is too slow at scale, so we approximate.
Approximate nearest neighbor indexes trade recall for speed.
Popular index types include HNSW graphs and IVF lists.
You tune parameters to balance latency against accuracy.
Always measure recall on your own data before shipping.
Thanks for watching, and see you in the next session.
